generated_oml:
import DTDFVocab
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for point cloud data collection",
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoUST10LXLiDARPointCloudData
]
instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera for depth data collection",
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthImageData
]
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]
instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthImageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
]
instance HokuyoPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud data used for object detection",
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoUST10LXLiDARPointCloudData,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]
instance DepthImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Depth data from Intel Deepsense D435i camera",
    DTDFVocab:fromData IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthImageData,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN and Kalman filter algorithms for object detection and tracking",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler
]
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based point cloud clustering and tracking using DBSCAN and Kalman filter",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]
instance ROS2Communication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for low-latency communication between physical and digital components",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]
instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for structured data storage and reporting",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]
instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data serialization, message type alignment, and unit conversion for Wi-Fi UDP communication between entities",
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]
instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink for model-based design and offloading of ACC logic",
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]
instance PythonCPlusPlusEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python and C++ for data processing and control algorithm implementation",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state, driving paths, and detected object tracks for reporting and analysis",
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackingInsight
]
instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for transferring ACC processing tasks to external resources",
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputationOffloadingAction
]
instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote control of ACC enable/disable, desired speed setting, and emergency braking commands",
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteEnableAction, RemoteDisableAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
]
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time ego vehicle speed and state data"
]
instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected objects with tracking information"
]
instance ComputationOffloadingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloading ACC processing to external resources for lead vehicle detection, speed computation, and actuation commands",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]
instance RemoteEnableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable ACC functionality remotely",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance RemoteDisableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Disable ACC functionality remotely",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set desired speed for ACC system",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issue emergency braking command remotely",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for vehicle speed control"
]
instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering Servo for vehicle steering control"
]
instance Motor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor for vehicle propulsion"
]
instance SpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed command for vehicle control"
]
instance SteeringCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering angle command for vehicle control"
]
instance MotorVoltageCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor voltage command for propulsion control"
]
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VedderElectronicSpeedController, Motor, SteeringServo
]
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
]
instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VisualizationComponent, ControlPanelComponent
]
instance VisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "3D Visualization interface for real-time data display"
]
instance ControlPanelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Control Panel for manual input and system control"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands (enable/disable ACC, modify desired speed) are initiated via the Control Panel User Interface FE in the User Entity. The ACC Controlling FE processes these commands to compute required speed, followed by the Data Translation component for serialization and unit conversion. The Actuation FE converts the desired speed into motor voltage commands for physical actuation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy for computation offloading between devices in safety-critical vehicular applications, as dictated by hardware limitations and industry standards."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system architecture follows ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA), with software components mapped to Functional Entities (FEs) implemented as individual ROS2 packages. Each package contains one or more ROS2 nodes. The entities are structured into Physical Twin Entity (handling data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication), Digital Twin Entity (data management and control), and User Entity (3D Visualization and Control Panel), each deployed on separate hardware components. The Physical Twin Entity deviates from ISO 23247 RA by encompassing general automotive functionality (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) rather than being limited to resource-specific OME."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Communication between software components uses ROS2 network, and inter-entity communication occurs over Wi-Fi using UDP transport layer. Network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, reliability mechanisms, and security protocols are not detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment uses edge hardware including NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer and NVIDIA Jetson NX antenna on the physical vehicle. Computation offloading is implemented between the Digital Twin and physical entities, with components distributed across different hardware platforms for scalability and real-time processing."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Compliance with ISO 23247:2021 standard, which includes four parts: Overview and general principles, Reference architecture, Digital representation of physical manufacturing elements, and Information exchange. The reference architecture (Part 2) defines an entity-based structure comprising Observable Manufacturing Elements (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity, each composed of Functional Entities (FEs) responsible for specific tasks such as data transformation into human-interpretable formats and user interaction interfaces."
]
